title: cli: `--volumepath` is not functional when backend is initialized.
body:
### Issue Description

Podman's `--volumepath` is not use able in regular setup

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman --volumepath <somepath> volume create testvol` // should create volume in `somepath`
2.  `podman --volumepath /tmp info | grep volumePath` // should show `/tmp` in volume path


### Describe the results you received

`--volumepath` is no-op

### Describe the results you expected

`--volumepath` should be functional

### Upstream Latest Release

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I think PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18431 should fix this, but lets see what other maintainers think.

Since its decided here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18431#issuecomment-1533022500 that this is expected, my proposal is to detect and error out instead of being option a `no-op` and update the docs accordingly. @mheon @Luap99 WDYT ?

Erroring out in this case is a big breaking change, updating docs SGTM.
There are already some logrus lines when the overwrite happens. I could be convinced to make them warnings.
I don't know why they're not warnings already, honestly
Yes make this a warning, so we don't have a breaking change.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
